focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 4

The Becker County deputies had made seven trips up and down the stairs. From the kitchen table, Joel counted every one. He'd been planted there since quarter to eight with a mug Paul kept refilling, and on each pass the deputies skipped the third step near the top. Paul had warned them about the creak before Joel even thought to mention it.

Cup four sat half-finished in front of him. Too much coffee on top of no sleep and no food had his hands shaking in short fast bursts. His stomach had gone sour an hour ago. Every time a deputy passed behind him, the muscles between his shoulders locked. He'd seen clients go that still in arraignment. He had never thought about what it cost them. Now he knew. It felt like being made small in public.

The great room still smelled like Martin's scotch, the Lagavulin he'd poured too generously last night, because Martin never knew how to pour lightly, host reflex still firing even when people told him to sit down. The draft board stayed on the wall, Martin's picks circled in red Sharpie. Nobody had touched it because touching it would mean deciding what it was now: a record, an artifact, evidence. Morning light came through the A-frame windows in thick gold slabs, postcard light, the wrong light for this.

Tommy stood on the deck with both hands around a coffee mug like it was the only solid thing left. A gray clammy cast had settled over his face, the look of a man bracing for numbers he already knew were bad. His right hand kept drifting to the pocket of his zip jacket and coming back out empty. In the kitchen doorway, Nate was explaining the lake house's history to a deputy who had not asked and did not care. Doug sat on the couch in the corner with his hood up and his knees pulled in, looking at his phone with the blan...
open_hooks_to_carry: Tommy's guilt about the Viagra — played for dark comedy, not melodrama
Tommy's clumsy tampering: taking the pills, dropping one in the hallway, flushing them
Mallick's arrival audible through the bathroom door — Tommy is trapped with evidence as the detective enters
Tommy's physicality under stress — pacing, gripping things, the coaching-mode body in a civilian crisis

payoffs_to_land.description: The joke from Ch 1 becomes Tommy's nightmare — he believes the Viagra triggered Martin's heart failure

prev_tail_excerpt:
...vastating coffee and stood at the kitchen window. The lake showed through the pines, iron-gray in the predawn light. The Adirondack chairs on the deck were wet with dew. The propane grill sat where Martin had made burgers eighteen hours ago, scraping the grates clean, scolding Tommy for leaving grease on the drip tray, everybody laughing. Eighteen hours ago.

"Deck."

Tommy was in the kitchen doorway. Showered, dressed in jeans and a polo shirt that was wrong for Tommy, too neat, too deliberate. His face was scrubbed raw. His hands were jammed in his pockets so deep the denim pulled tight, and he stood too still for a man that size.

"We should call," Tommy said. "Should've called hours ago."

"Yeah."

"I'll do it. I'll call from the deck, there's better signal."

"OK."

Tommy stayed there another beat, gave Paul a look Paul would spend weeks failing to name, then went out the sliding door. Paul heard his voice on the deck, his coaching voice, reporting the death of Martin Kessler to the Becker County 911 dispatcher with the clipped factual cadence of a man who had called in injuries before: name, location, situation, time discovered. He'd stripped the emotion out of it. Through the glass Paul could see Tommy's knuckles whitening on the railing.

Paul looked around the kitchen. The fluorescent tube buzzed. Morning came through the pines in thin gold lines and laid them across the lake.

The sun cleared the tree line. Paul poured out his cold coffee, rinsed the mug, and set it on the wooden drying rack Martin had once hauled back from Fargo like it mattered. Then he stood at the sink with water running over his hands and cried soundlessly while Tommy's voice carried through the glass door, giving directions to a house none of them would ever walk into the same way again.
secondary_character_beats: At least one moment where Tommy's coaching instincts surface inappropriately — he tries to manage the crisis like a halftime talk and it fails
state_deltas_required: Tommy's conviction that the Viagra he gave Martin contributed to the cardiac arrest
Tommy has destroyed evidence linking him to a death he didn't cause, and the detective is now in the house
